abstract: Exposure to air pollution represents a critical risk factor for a wide range of life-threatening diseases, making the development of robust forecasting systems essential for public health. Such mechanisms enable authorities to implement preemptive measures when elevated pollutant concentrations are anticipated. In recent years, Machine Learning—and specifically Deep Learning—has emerged as a dominant approach for predicting air quality. This paper provides a comprehensive systematic review of the primary contributions to this field between 2011 and 2021. Following a rigorous selection process across major scientific databases, 155 relevant studies were identified and analyzed. These papers are categorized and discussed based on their geographical focus, target pollutants, predictor variables, evaluation metrics, and the specific Machine Learning architectures employed.
